name: Riley
manager_notes: Riley is in week two and is moving quickly through codebase orientation, but he has mostly learned from docs and async notes rather than direct team contact. I want to check whether he feels clear on ownership boundaries, incident expectations, and where to ask for context before making architecture assumptions. There is a risk he looks productive while building an internal map that differs from how the team actually operates. This check-in should identify missing context early and convert it into concrete onboarding support.
answers: Comfortable reading the services, but less sure how decisions are really made here.
Can trace most request paths now, but still misses historical context behind certain tradeoffs.
Not fully clear who's on point for each domain when priorities conflict.
Tries not to interrupt people → keeps questions in a notes doc instead of asking.
Would benefit from a clearer guide on what to escalate quickly versus batch.
Runbook helped, but some practical handoff details only show up during live incidents.
A short weekly architecture review with someone senior would help calibrate faster.
Knowing exactly what ownership to take this month would build confidence.
Will ask one architecture question in the next incident review instead of keeping it in notes.
